issues.title: docs/architecture.md tree: `resources/` comment column off by one space
issues.description: docs/architecture.md:140 — in the project-side `.gogo/` tree, the `#` comment on the new `resources/` line starts one column left of its siblings. `knowledge/` and `skills/` align their `#` at the same column, but `resources/` (same 10-char name, same tree depth) is one space earlier, so the comment column no longer lines up. Cosmetic only; no behavioural impact.
issues.proposed_solution: Add one space before the `#` on docs/architecture.md:140 so the `resources/` comment aligns with the `knowledge/` and `skills/` comment column.
issues.fix_summary: Added one space before the # on the resources/ tree line in docs/architecture.md so its comment column aligns with knowledge/ and skills/.

issues.title: Migration log says "already current (no-op)" in the partial-migration (both .gogo/plans and .gogo/work present) case
issues.description: skills/gogo-build/SKILL.md Step 0 — when both `.gogo/plans/` and `.gogo/work/` already exist (the partial-migration conflict the prose itself says to "flag in the report for a human to merge"), the bash skips the move (correct, no-clobber) and, if nothing else moves, the final line echoes `migration: already current (no-op)`. That contradicts the prose flag and FR3's explicit "logged" requirement, and conflicts with the standards' "no silent failures" bar: an operator reading that line could miss that a legacy `.gogo/plans/` still sits there needing a manual merge. The data is safe (move-never-delete), but the log misreports a real conflict as success.
issues.proposed_solution: In the Step 0 illustrative bash, detect the both-exist conflict and warn instead of declaring no-op, e.g. after the moves: `if [ -d .gogo/plans ] && [ -e .gogo/work ]; then echo "WARN: legacy .gogo/plans/ remains alongside .gogo/work/ — partial migration, merge manually"; fi`, and make the no-op branch fire only when `.gogo/plans/` is genuinely absent. Keep the Step 7 report surfacing the same flag.
issues.fix_summary: Reworked the Step 0 final log into if/elif/else: prints 'migrated: ...' when moves happened; 'WARN: legacy .gogo/plans/ remains alongside .gogo/work/ ... merge by hand, then re-run' when both still exist (the partial-migration conflict); 'already current (no-op)' only when .gogo/plans/ is genuinely absent.

issues.title: Chart-kind enum left out of sync: contracts docs still list the 4-kind set, missing `use-case`
issues.description: FR6 extended `templates/contracts/charts-manifest.schema.json` to `"enum": ["flow","sequence","class","activity","use-case"]` and broadened the `file` description to allow `report/` paths. But two prose descriptions of that same contract were not updated: docs/contracts.md:55-56 and templates/contracts/README.md:55-56 both still state `each diagram { kind ∈ {flow, sequence, class, activity}, file (a .mmd under charts/), title }`. This is an enumeration left out of sync (code-review-standards #1 'Cross-file consistency … grep the old terms'; severity guide: 'Major — an enumeration left out of sync'). A reader of the contracts doc/README would conclude `use-case` is an invalid kind and that `.mmd` files only live under `charts/`, directly contradicting the schema those very docs describe. The machine-enforced schema is correct, so there is no runtime break, but the contract documentation now disagrees with the contract.
issues.proposed_solution: In both docs/contracts.md:55-56 and templates/contracts/README.md:55-56, change the enum to `{flow, sequence, class, activity, use-case}` and broaden the file note to `a .mmd under charts/ (or report/ for the ⑤ as-built report bundle)`. While there, note that report ⑤ also emits a `report/manifest.json` governed by the same schema (currently both lines say only 'Implement ② emits the as-built set').
issues.fix_summary: Synced the chart-kind enum in both contract docs (docs/contracts.md + templates/contracts/README.md): kind now {flow, sequence, class, activity, use-case}, file note broadened to 'charts/ (or report/ for the ⑤ bundle)', and both note report ⑤ emits report/manifest.json under the same schema. Matches charts-manifest.schema.json.

issues.title: charts-manifest.schema.json top-level description omits the new ⑤ report/manifest.json reuse
issues.description: templates/contracts/charts-manifest.schema.json:5 still frames the artifact as 'An index of the as-built diagram set implement (②) emitted into charts/, so review (③) and test (④) can find and reason about the diagrams …'. As of FR4/FR6 the same schema also governs `report/manifest.json` written at phase ⑤ (gogo-knowledge's validate-out validates it against this schema, and /gogo:done copies it). The per-property `file` description was updated to mention `report/`, but the top-level `description` was not, so the schema's self-documentation under-states its scope. Cosmetic/clarity only — the schema validates report manifests correctly.
issues.proposed_solution: Broaden the top-level `description` in templates/contracts/charts-manifest.schema.json to note the same index is also written by report ⑤ as `report/manifest.json` (the as-built report bundle), not only by implement ② into `charts/`.
issues.fix_summary: Broadened the charts-manifest.schema.json top-level description to note report ⑤ also emits report/manifest.json under the same schema (archived by /gogo:done), not only implement ② into charts/.

issues.title: One invalid diagram blanks ALL diagrams: mermaid.run lacks suppressErrors and the single catch replaces every pre.mermaid
issues.description: assets/viewer/interactive.js:125-127 — render is one batched call: `Promise.resolve(window.mermaid.run({ querySelector: "pre.mermaid" }))...catch(function (err) { fail(...) })`. mermaid `run()` defaults to `suppressErrors:false`, so it rejects on the FIRST diagram that fails to parse; the single `.catch` then calls `fail()`, which iterates `document.querySelectorAll("pre.mermaid")` and replaces EVERY remaining mermaid block (including the valid ones) with the same error message. Net effect: one malformed `.mmd` in a report bundle takes down every diagram on the page, with no per-diagram graceful degradation. This undercuts the FR10 'degrades gracefully' bar — a multi-diagram report should still show its good diagrams. Verified by reading the control flow (the catch is global, fail() is global). Note the inlined `.mmd` sources come straight from the report bundle, so an authoring slip in any one diagram is a realistic trigger.
issues.proposed_solution: Pass `suppressErrors: true` to run (`window.mermaid.run({ querySelector: "pre.mermaid", suppressErrors: true })`) so mermaid renders the good diagrams and inserts its own inline error graphic for the bad one, then proceed to setupViewport. Keep the top-level `!window.mermaid` fail() path (whole-runtime-missing) but scope render failures per-figure rather than blanking the whole page. Optionally wrap each setupViewport in try/catch so one viewport that can't initialise doesn't abort the others.
issues.fix_summary: Added suppressErrors:true to mermaid.run so a single malformed .mmd renders its own inline error instead of rejecting the batch; the global fail() is now reserved for the missing-runtime case. Good diagrams still render + get viewports.

issues.title: Root-layout (pre-Stage-2) reports are selectable but render with NO diagrams — Step 3 only scans the bundle dir, not charts/
issues.description: skills/gogo-view/SKILL.md:47 deliberately adds legacy root-level reports to the picker (`ls .gogo/work/feature-*/report.md`, comment: 'include those too'), and Step 3 (SKILL.md:98) gathers diagrams from 'each `*.mmd` in the bundle dir'. For the new `report/` layout and copied changelog entries the `.mmd` files sit beside `report.md`, so that works. But for the legacy root layout the bundle dir is the feature ROOT, while those features keep their diagrams under `charts/` (a sibling subdir, per the plan's 'Context — charts in charts/'). Result: selecting a legacy report yields a silent summary-only page — the viewer's headline feature (interactive diagrams) is missing for a class of reports the skill itself offers, with no warning. It does not crash (so the 'at least not crash' bar holds), but it is a silent data omission for a supported pick.
issues.proposed_solution: In Step 3, when the chosen report.md is at a feature root (legacy layout), also gather `*.mmd` from the sibling `charts/` dir; OR drop root-layout reports from the picker (Step 1) so only fully-supported bundles are offered; OR, minimally, when a selected bundle has no `.mmd`, emit a one-line note in the skill's Return so the user knows the page is summary-only by design rather than by mistake.
issues.fix_summary: gogo-view Step 3 now gathers diagrams by layout: report/ bundle (or changelog entry) -> *.mmd beside report.md; legacy root report.md -> the feature's charts/*.mmd. If none found for the pick, it tells the user 'showing the summary only' so a missing diagram set is never silent.

issues.title: Clicking a floating control button also starts a (zero-distance) viewport drag + setPointerCapture
issues.description: assets/viewer/interactive.js — the `.controls` div is appended as a child of `viewport` (line 114), and the drag handler is registered on `viewport` (pointerdown, line 80). Pointer events bubble, so pressing a control button (−/+/fit/reset) fires the viewport's pointerdown: it sets `drag = {...}`, adds the `grabbing` cursor class, and calls `viewport.setPointerCapture(e.pointerId)` on the viewport during the button press. The click still works (no movement → tx/ty unchanged, endDrag clears it), so this is not a functional break, but it causes a needless grab-cursor flicker on every control click and puts a pointer capture on the ancestor mid-button-press, which is fragile across browsers. Verified by reading the DOM wiring (controls are inside the captured element).
issues.proposed_solution: Ignore pointerdowns that originate in the controls: at the top of the viewport pointerdown handler add `if (e.target.closest('.controls')) return;`, or call `ctl.addEventListener('pointerdown', function (e) { e.stopPropagation(); })` so button presses never reach the pan handler.
issues.fix_summary: Added `if (e.target.closest('.controls')) return;` at the top of the viewport pointerdown handler so control-button clicks no longer start a zero-distance drag / setPointerCapture on the ancestor.

issues.title: GOGO_VIEW_TITLE guidance produces a doubled "report —" and unstripped markdown backticks in the <title>
issues.description: skills/gogo-view/SKILL.md:83 defines GOGO_VIEW_TITLE as 'the report's title (its first `# ` heading, e.g. `gogo report — <slug>`)'. The example implies prefixing 'gogo report — ', but a report's first heading is itself e.g. 'Report — feature `<slug>`', and the markdown inline code (backticks) is not stripped. The generated sample (.gogo/resources/view/docs-and-verified-discovery.html:6) shows the result: `<title>gogo report — Report — feature \`docs-and-verified-discovery\`</title>` — a redundant 'report —' and literal backticks visible in the browser tab. The sample is an artifact (not committed source), but the defect traces to the skill's title instruction. Cosmetic only.
issues.proposed_solution: Clarify SKILL.md:83: derive the title from the first `# ` heading's TEXT with inline markdown stripped (drop backticks/`*`), and do not re-prefix 'gogo report —' when the heading already begins with 'Report'. e.g. 'use the first `# ` heading as plain text (strip ` ` `*` `_`); if it doesn't already start with gogo/Report, prefix "gogo report — ".'
issues.fix_summary: Reworded the GOGO_VIEW_TITLE token guidance to produce a clean plain-text tab title 'gogo — <name>' (date-slug or slug), markdown stripped (no backticks/#/**), and no duplicated 'report'.

issues.title: docs/flow.md "## The five phases" section now contains seven subsections (the 5 phases + Ship + View)
issues.description: docs/flow.md:31 — the H2 heading reads `## The five phases`, but this FR11 pass added two new H3 subsections under it: `### Ship — command /gogo:done` (line 77) and `### View — command /gogo:view` (line 86), alongside the five phase subsections ①–⑤ (lines 33-62). So a section explicitly titled 'five phases' now enumerates seven items, and `/gogo:done` and `/gogo:view` are documented as if they were pipeline phases when they are explicitly NOT phases (the plan's Out-of-scope keeps the phase set at five; FR7/FR8 add them as post-pipeline commands). This is a verified cross-file/labelling inconsistency (code-review-standards #1 — 'No place still describes the old behaviour'; severity guide — Minor: wording drift / an example that no longer matches). For contrast, the parallel section in skills/gogo/SKILL.md:115 uses the count-free heading `## The phases` and reads fine with the same Ship/View additions, so the fix is to align flow.md to that. No behavioural impact — purely a docs structure nit. Verified by reading the heading tree of both files.
issues.proposed_solution: Rename docs/flow.md:31 from `## The five phases` to a count-free heading that accommodates the post-pipeline commands — e.g. `## The phases` (to match skills/gogo/SKILL.md), or keep the five-phase framing and lift `### Ship` and `### View` out under a separate H2 such as `## After the pipeline — ship & view`. Either removes the 'five' vs seven-subsections mismatch.
issues.fix_summary: Renamed docs/flow.md H2 'The five phases' → 'The phases' (matching skills/gogo/SKILL.md), so the heading no longer conflicts with the seven nested H3s (five phases + Ship/View post-pipeline commands).
